pages.raw_text:
Spokane is the cleanest city we
have ever seen"


Such is the concensus of opinion
of the 100 homeseekers who arrived
in the city today in charge of f. G..
Cross, assistant general immigration
agent for the Great Northern Rail-
way company.


The homeseekers arrived shortly
after Z o'clock this morning For
more than two hours and a half they
were conveyed to every nook and
corner of the city where a streey
car could take them.


On their arrival here they were
taken in charge by the chamber OF
commerce, and Secretary Monroe or
dered that they be shown every cour-
tesy.


SO pleased were a number of the
immigrants and homeseekers with
Spokane that. although they had In
tended to go on to the coast, they
will remain in Spokane to look
around little"


This city is good enough for me, "
said several. and instead of going
on to some other portion of the state,
remained here. The remaining por-
ton of the party left shortly after
10 o'clock for the coast or the Big
Bend country. where some intend to
take homesteads or buy land,
